skill.name: hugging-face-cli
skill.description: Execute Hugging Face Hub operations using the hf CLI for downloading models, uploading files, managing repositories, and running compute jobs.
skill.summary: Interact with Hugging Face Hub directly from the terminal using the official hf CLI tool.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 76 patterns in documentation content, but all are false positives. The skill file is markdown documentation showing usage examples for the official Hugging Face hf CLI tool, not executable code. No actual security risks exist - external command patterns are CLI documentation examples, network references are URLs in documentation, and cryptographic warnings do not match any actual crypto implementations.

content.user_title: Manage Hugging Face Hub from the Terminal
content.value_statement: Working with AI models and datasets on Hugging Face Hub requires multiple tools and manual steps. This skill streamlines the workflow by providing direct CLI access to download, upload, and manage ML resources through unified commands.
actual_capabilities: Download models, datasets, and spaces from Hugging Face Hub to local storage
Upload files and directories to Hugging Face repositories with commit messages
Create, delete, and manage Hugging Face repositories with visibility settings
Manage local cache of downloaded models and datasets
Browse and search the Hugging Face Hub for models, datasets, and spaces
Run GPU compute jobs on Hugging Face infrastructure
limitations: Requires hf CLI tool to be installed and authenticated on the system
Cannot perform inference or model training directly through CLI commands
Repository operations require valid Hugging Face authentication token
Large model downloads depend on available local storage and network bandwidth

use_cases.title: ML Engineer Model Deployment
use_cases.description: Download pre-trained models from Hugging Face Hub for local deployment and inference serving.

use_cases.title: Researcher Dataset Management
use_cases.description: Upload experimental datasets to private repositories and share with collaborators through versioned releases.

use_cases.title: Developer Cache Optimization
use_cases.description: Manage local model cache to optimize storage and quickly access frequently used models for development.

prompt_templates.title: Download a Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Download the model meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct to my local models directory.

prompt_templates.title: Upload Files to Hub
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload my trained model from the ./output folder to my Hugging Face repository with the commit message 'Initial model release'.

prompt_templates.title: Search for Datasets
prompt_templates.prompt: Find datasets related to text classification with high download counts and show me their details.

prompt_templates.title: Manage Cache and Run Compute Job
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my current cache usage, remove unused models, then run a GPU job to process my dataset using the specified image and command.

output_examples.input: Download a model to local directory
output_examples.output: Model meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct downloaded successfully to ./models directory. Total size: 2.1 GB across 15 files.
Cached repositories: gpt2 (1.2 GB), bert-base-uncased (440 MB), t5-base (890 MB). Total cache usage: 2.53 GB.

best_practices: Always use the --quiet flag when you only need the download path for scripting purposes
Create private repositories for sensitive models and datasets before uploading proprietary content
Use commit messages that clearly describe changes when uploading model updates
Regularly prune detached cache revisions to free up disk space
anti_patterns: Do not upload files containing API keys, credentials, or sensitive configuration data to public repositories
Avoid downloading entire large models without checking available disk space first
Do not share your HF_TOKEN in command history or commit messages
Avoid running compute jobs without estimating costs using the --flavor option

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Hugging Face Hub?
faq.answer: Run hf auth login for interactive authentication, or use hf auth login --token YOUR_TOKEN for non-interactive login with a personal access token from your Hugging Face account settings.

faq.question: Where are downloaded models stored?
faq.answer: Models are stored in the Hugging Face cache directory by default. Use the --local-dir option to specify a custom download location, or run hf cache ls to see cached items.

faq.question: Can I upload to someone else's repository?
faq.answer: You can only upload to repositories you own or have write access to. For contributing to other projects, use the --create-pr flag to submit a pull request instead.

faq.question: How do I manage large model files?
faq.answer: Use the --include flag to download only specific file types like *.safetensors. For uploads, consider using Git LFS which the hf CLI handles automatically for large files.

faq.question: What GPU options are available for compute jobs?
faq.answer: Available flavors range from CPU-basic to H100 GPUs. Use --flavor with values like t4-small, l4x1, a10g-small, or a100-large depending on your compute requirements and budget.

faq.question: How do I check my authentication status?
faq.answer: Run hf auth whoami to see your current logged-in user, or hf auth list to see all stored authentication tokens on your system.
